title: Finding the median from a back-to-back stem plot
problem:
A back-to-back stem-and-leaf plot shows the number of hours per week two groups of students spend on hobbies.

  Group P  | Stem | Group Q
       6   |  1   | 2  5  8
   9  5  2 |  2   | 0  4  7
   7  4    |  3   | 1  6  9
       3   |  4   | 5

Leaves for Group P are read from the stem outward (right to left).

Find the median for Group P.

steps.instruction: Read the Group P leaves for each stem and write out the values.
steps.working:
Stem 1, leaf 6 → 16
Stem 2, leaves 2, 5, 9 (outward from stem) → 22, 25, 29
Stem 3, leaves 4, 7 (outward) → 34, 37
Stem 4, leaf 3 → 43

steps.instruction: Write the values in ascending order and count them.

steps.working:
For $n = 7$ values, the median is the $\dfrac{7 + 1}{2} = 4$th value.
4th value = 29

answer: The median for Group P is 29 hours.

title: Finding median, mode and range from a back-to-back stem plot
problem:
A back-to-back stem-and-leaf plot shows the number of points scored by players in two sports teams.

  Team X   | Stem | Team Y
   8  6  4 |  2   | 1  3  5
   9  5  3 |  3   | 0  4  8
   6  6  1 |  4   | 2  6  9
       5   |  5   | 1  4

Leaves for Team X are read from the stem outward (right to left).

For Team X, find: (a) the median, (b) the mode, and (c) the range.

steps.instruction: List the Team X values in ascending order.

steps.working:
10 values → median is the average of the 5th and 6th values.
5th value = 35,  6th value = 39
Median $= \dfrac{35 + 39}{2} = 37$

Scan for repeated values: 46 appears twice; all other values appear once.
Mode = 46

title: Comparing two distributions using a back-to-back stem plot
problem:
A back-to-back stem-and-leaf plot shows the marks (out of 80) achieved by two exam classes.

  Team A   | Stem | Team B
       8   |  3   | 1  4  6
   9  7  5 |  4   | 0  3  8
   8  6  4 |  5   | 2  5  9
   9  7  3 |  6   | 1  7
       5   |  7   | 4  8

Leaves for Team A are read from the stem outward (right to left).

Use the median and range to compare the performance of the two classes.

steps.instruction: List the Team A values in ascending order and find the median and range.
List the Team B values in ascending order and find the median and range.

steps.working:
Team A median = 56,  Team B median = 52.
Team A has a higher median, suggesting Team A generally achieved higher marks.

Team A range = 37,  Team B range = 47.
Team B has a greater range, indicating more variation in Team B's results.

answer: Team A has a higher median mark (56 compared to 52), suggesting Team A generally performed better. Team B has a greater range (47 compared to 37), indicating more spread in Team B's results.
